task:
Phase C — Reviewer role (pre-ship judge gate) for the Foreman orchestrator (repo: my-pi-harness, extension: extensions/foreman).

GOAL: Add a code-review seat between "per-round verification passed" and "Gate 2 (ship)". Today (index.ts ~line 1107) when successState === "success" there is a `// TODO(Phase C): run declared pre-ship gates here before opening Gate 2.` — implement that hook. Pre-ship gates run ONCE, after the per-round dev->test->fix loop has gone green, before the founder is asked to ship. A pre-ship JUDGE gate spawns the `reviewer` agent; a pre-ship COMMAND gate (e.g. e2e) runs via the existing runCommandGates(gates, "pre-ship", ...). If any pre-ship gate fails, the loop REOPENS another dev round (feeding the reviewer's diagnosis back to the developer) instead of going to Gate 2 — exactly like a per-round FAIL, but triggered by pre-ship checks.

CONTEXT YOU MUST READ FIRST (do real recon — the guard now blocks main-session edits, so this WILL run as a foreman crew task):
- extensions/foreman/index.ts — the orchestrator round loop. Study: how the tester is spawned (loadAgent("tester"), runAgent(tester, testerTask, ...), parseVerdict(testRun.text)); the successState decision block; the `// TODO(Phase C)` site at ~1107-1108; the per-round FAIL path that builds devContext for the next round; how runCommandGates + gatesForStage("pre-ship") already exist from Phase A.
- extensions/foreman/gates.ts — Gate type, kinds (command|judge|action), stages (per-round|pre-ship|release), gatesForStage, hasStage, runCommandGates. The reviewer is a stage:"pre-ship" kind:"judge" gate (agent:"reviewer").
- extensions/foreman/crew/tester.md — the judge crew-file format + VERDICT machine block contract. The reviewer mirrors this shape but reviews code QUALITY, not test pass/fail.
- extensions/foreman/planner.ts — the pure-helper module pattern (node-builtins only, headlessly testable). Add reviewer parsing/decision helpers in the same style (either extend an existing module or add extensions/foreman/reviewer.ts — your call, keep it pure).

DELIVERABLES:
1. NEW crew file extensions/foreman/crew/reviewer.md
   - frontmatter: name: reviewer; model: cliproxy/claude-opus-4-8:xhigh; tools: read, grep, find, ls, bash (READ-ONLY — same posture as tester; the reviewer MUST NOT edit/write/fix code, only judge. Bash is read-only inspection like `git diff`).
   - system prompt: a senior staff-engineer code reviewer. It reviews the DIFF of the work (use `git diff` to see what changed) for: correctness beyond tests, security (injection, secrets, unsafe shell/`spawn`, path traversal), maintainability, architecture/consistency with the codebase, scope creep, missing error handling at real boundaries. It does NOT re-run the test suite (the tester + command gates already did). Distinguish blocking issues from nits.
   - MANDATORY machine block: a VERDICT line exactly parseable, mirroring the tester contract but with review semantics:
       REVIEW: APPROVE   (ship-ready; no blocking issues)
       REVIEW: REQUEST-CHANGES  (blocking issues; must loop back to developer)
     Followed, when REQUEST-CHANGES, by a `BLOCKING:` section with concrete `file:line - what to change` bullets the developer can act on without re-investigating. Nits go in a separate non-blocking `NITS:` section that does NOT trigger a reopen.

2. PURE HELPER (node-builtins only, headlessly testable — this is the verify target's core):
   - parseReviewVerdict(text): returns { decision: "approve" | "request-changes" | "unknown", blocking: string[], nits: string[] }. Deterministic regex on the REVIEW: line, same robustness as parseVerdict (case-insensitive, tolerant of surrounding prose). Unknown/missing => treat as a SAFE default: do NOT auto-approve (an unparseable review must NOT silently ship — default to letting the founder decide; see wiring).
   - A small decision helper if useful (e.g. given the parsed review + whether a reviewer gate is even configured, decide reopen vs proceed-to-gate2).

3. WIRE into index.ts at the TODO(Phase C) site (minimal blast radius; do NOT touch the ActivityPhase union, dashboard, ledger schema, gates.ts, or guard.ts):
   - When successState === "success": BEFORE opening Gate 2, run pre-ship gates IF gatesForStage(gates, "pre-ship") is non-empty.
     a) pre-ship COMMAND gates: runCommandGates(gates, "pre-ship", cwd, signal). Any non-zero exit => treat as a fail => reopen a dev round (append the failing gate name+output to devContext), same machinery as the per-round fail path. Respect maxRounds (a pre-ship failure consuming the last round escalates, like today).
     b) pre-ship JUDGE gates (kind:"judge", e.g. agent:"reviewer"): for each, loadAgent(gate.agent), spawn via runAgent with a reviewer task that tells it to `git diff` and judge; parse with parseReviewVerdict. REQUEST-CHANGES => reopen a dev round feeding the BLOCKING bullets back to the developer (NITS are surfaced to the founder but do NOT reopen). APPROVE => continue.
   - Only when ALL pre-ship gates pass/approve do we proceed to the existing Gate 2 (ship) prompt. Surface a concise pre-ship summary (gate names + pass/approve, plus any NITS) into the Gate 2 message so the founder sees the review result.
   - SAFETY/BACKWARD COMPAT: if there are NO pre-ship gates configured (the default today, since most repos have no foreman.json or no pre-ship entries), behavior is IDENTICAL to now — straight to Gate 2. An unparseable/`unknown` reviewer verdict must NOT auto-approve and must NOT infinite-loop: treat unknown as a non-blocking "could not review" that proceeds to Gate 2 but clearly flags to the founder that review was inconclusive (do NOT reopen on unknown — that would risk burning all rounds on a flaky parse). Document this choice in a comment.
   - Persist each pre-ship reviewer result as a handoff (reuse writeHandoff with role — note: Handoff.role is currently "developer"|"tester"; to avoid a ledger schema change, record the reviewer handoff as role "tester" with a clear summary prefix like "[reviewer] ...", OR write a log event via appendLog. Pick the lower-blast-radius option that does NOT change the Handoff type, and document it.) Add appendLog events for pre-ship gate runs + reviewer verdicts so the dashboard log trail shows them.
   - Reviewer spawn is a cliproxy/Anthropic agent => append-only system prompt via the existing runAgent path (quota-safe). NEVER a replace-style system prompt.

STRICT CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT change: ActivityPhase union, Handoff type/ledger schema, gates.ts, guard.ts, the dashboard.
- The reviewer is READ-ONLY (enforce via tools frontmatter; no write/edit).
- A reviewer/pre-ship failure must respect maxRounds and never infinite-loop. An unparseable reviewer verdict must never silently ship NOR loop forever (proceed-to-Gate-2-flagged, per above).
- Keep ALL existing test suites passing. The default no-pre-ship-gates path must be byte-for-byte behaviorally identical to today (existing gate_flow_test.sh must still pass).

TEST (create + make this the verify target): extensions/foreman/test/reviewer_test.sh — headless node test, same style as planner_test.sh / gates_test.sh. Since spawning a real reviewer model is out of scope for a unit test, test the PURE helpers + decision logic:
- parseReviewVerdict: "REVIEW: APPROVE" => approve; "REVIEW: REQUEST-CHANGES" + a BLOCKING list => request-changes with the bullets captured; NITS captured separately and never treated as blocking; a response with no REVIEW line => unknown (NOT approve).
- decision logic: request-changes => reopen; approve => proceed; unknown => proceed-but-flagged (NOT reopen, NOT silent-ship).
- a pre-ship gate selection test: gatesForStage(gates,"pre-ship") correctly picks command+judge pre-ship gates and ignores per-round/release ones (you may import gates.ts for this).
Also run the existing suites in the verify command so nothing regressed.

End with the mandatory DEV-JSON machine block.
verifyCommand: bash extensions/foreman/test/reviewer_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/planner_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/gates_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/guard_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/fallback_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/ledger_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh
